text: Dr. Craig Rogers demonstrates kidney mobilization and tumor exposure. The surgeon incises the Girotis fascia near the tumor, avoiding direct incision over it. The kidney is repositioned using the fourth arm and prograsp to achieve optimal tumor exposure, ensuring a head-on view with at least two centimeters of normal capsule surrounding the tumor for resection. The same steps are applied to the lower pole tumor.
The surgeon uses intraoperative ultrasound to define the boundaries of the kidney tumor, providing a detailed view to assist in planning the resection.
